rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Terminate Receivership
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/2024 to be heard by the same judicial officer. Need a verified written statement regarding the estate of Ivy Fong and the estate of Caroline Fong as to (1) if each of them died testate or intestate and (2) who the successors-in-interest are. At the hearing, the counsel of the personal rep orally reported that she believed Silvia and Diane were the successors of the estate of Caroline Fong; however, no such writing has been filed by any party. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need an authority under which the court can convert Petition for Final Distribution to Petition for Preliminary Distribution (or similar) by an attorney's declaration. 2. Attorney Dawkin's response to the OTA/OSC deferring the determination of Sarah Lum's residence to the court; however, that is not before the court. Petition for Probate must properly set forth the residence and file in an appropriate county at the counsel's legal review. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEXANDRA M. POST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/7/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Schedule G attached to the petition did not have a detailed itemization of the attorney costs attached. (3) The estimated costs of $600 should be part of the reserve, as the Court will not order payment of an uncertain amount of costs not yet incurred. (4) Did the two heirs have separate legal counsel or know that they were entitled to be separately represented and chose not to? (5) The allegation re Probate Code Section 9202(b) on page 2 is a little vague and refers to just one female petitioner. Did the co-administrators confirm that no heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc.? (6) The co-administrators allege that certain personal effects were of nominal value and were either distributed or donated/discarded, but all of the tangible personal property is to be distributed now? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. Probate Code Section 1065 does not exist. The second sentence of the paragraph regarding Character of Property of Estate on page 5 of the petition will be disregarded. The Court will not find and order that an unfiled claim was late or rejected or written off, etc. An unfiled claim does not exist for the Court.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Close Of The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/7/24. A Civil dismissal is not appropriate in a Probate estate. If the Inventory and Appraisal and a Petition for Final Distribution (or a report of status of administration) is not filed by 7/24/24, and set for a future hearing, the attorney and the personal representative must appear on 8/7/24. If there are no assets in the estate, after all, a petition must be filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 12251.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Authority To Remove Nonresident"S Property To Place Of Residence
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/20/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/29/2024 - see also tentative ruling for 05/28/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged (one previously uploaded was rejected by the clerk). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form on each financial institution at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified. The verification must include language required by CCP section 2015.5. 4) Each translation certificate must include language required by CCP section 2015.5. 5) The German court's certificate (consisting of the court's decision and letters of guardianship) with the original apostille(s) must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 6) The German court clerk's certification appears to have been made in 2016. Do the German Court' decision and letters of guardianship issued in 2016 continue to remain in effect on the same terms and conditions? 7) Please identify specific "financial management" powers the petitioner as guardian/conservator is granted under applicable German law with respect to the US bank accounts and annuity. 8) A copy of the application for the Wells Fargo account 1021 identifies the petitioner as a co-applicant. Is the petitioner a registered joint owner of the account? Please provide a copy of a statement of the account or other documentation showing how title is currently held. 9) Re the Wells Fargo accounts 7529 and 7593 titled under the name of the ward/conservatee's trust: i) Please provide a copy of the trust. ii) Is the petitioner the sole trustee currently acting? If not, please provide the names and addresses of all trustees to whom notice must be given. iii) Is the petitioner requesting the Court's determination that the accounts beneficially belong to the ward/conservatee individually, despite legal title held by the trustee(s)? If so, has the petitioner filed a petition under Probate Code section 850(a)(1)(D) or (a)(3)(A) by opening a new trust case (PTR) under the name of the trust? Note: An appearance may be required on 08/20/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of diligence, if papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are not filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE ERICH RUDASILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: OFF CALENDAR (request for dismissal filed)

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The "original" will is a photocopy. If the original will is lost, the petition must be amended to show that the petitioner seeks to admit a lost will, and the presumption of revocation must be overcome. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The petition caption is incomplete if IAEA authority is being requested. (5) Item 3f(2) of the petition has the wrong box checked and appears to be incomplete if an original will is located, as the photocopy of the will that was filed and attached to the petition shows that the will was self-proving. (6) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (7) Not all persons mentioned in the will are listed in item 8 of the petition, and they were also not given notice. (8) The petitioner alleges that the other named executors will not act because of death or declination, but no information about any death is provided and no declination has been filed or was attached to the petition. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/2/24. If the original will cannot be found (and filed), an amended petition for probate of a lost will must be filed and notice of the amended petition must be mailed and published. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH CHARLES LAWRENCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/16/2024 for sufficient time for the clerk's office to e-file Proof of publication. Also, the following issues must be cleared by filing a verified supplement (or supplements) two weeks prior: (1) identifying each person's relationship to the decedent. Petition #8 states several names and addresses, but missing relationships. (2) identifying the persons who are noticed. Multiple people were noticed, but Petition #8 does not set forth their information. If applicable, their info need to be listed on Petition #8. (3) Clarifying if the decedent was survived by a registered domestic partner and/or issue of a predeceased child. Petition #5.(3)-(4) and (7)-(8) not affirmed. (4) If David Evans is entitled to notice, the court may require a declaration of due diligent search. Due to missing information on David's relationship to the decedent, the examiner is unable to determine. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUNG HAO CHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/7/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Publication appears to have been in the Daily Journal, not West County Times.)
